paper_title: Investigating the consistency between proxy-based reconstructions and climate models using data assimilation: a mid-Holocene case study

methods.description: This method involves simulating mid-Holocene climate states using the LOVECLIM climate model by altering initial conditions and evaluating the resulting climate states against proxy-based reconstructions. The process aims to explore possible climate states and their agreement with historical data.

steps.description: Change initial conditions by adding small noise to the sea surface temperature while keeping physics and forcing unchanged.
steps.searchable_summary: alter initial conditions by adding noise to sea surface temperature

steps.description: Propagate 96 particles forward in time for six months using the LOVECLIM climate model.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate climate model with 96 particles for six months

steps.description: Evaluate 96 climate states according to their agreement with proxy-based reconstructions.
steps.searchable_summary: compare climate states with proxy-based reconstructions

steps.description: Resample particles based on likelihood to maintain a constant number of particles.
Add small perturbation to surface temperature and propagate particles for the next 6 months.
steps.searchable_summary: perturb surface temperature and simulate next 6 months

methods.description: This method involves performing and comparing mid-Holocene simulations with data assimilation using different sets of proxy-based reconstructions to understand the information each subset provides and their compatibility with model physics.

steps.description: Perform three mid-Holocene simulations with data assimilation using different sets of proxy-based reconstructions.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate mid-Holocene climate with different proxy sets

steps.description: Compare simulations to a mid-Holocene reference simulation named NODATA.
Analyze simulations performed with GCMs to compare LOVECLIM results with other models.

methods.description: This method involves selecting and preparing a data set of proxy-based reconstructions for use in climate simulations, focusing on records that represent air or sea surface temperature and meet specific criteria.

steps.description: Select proxy-based reconstructions representing air or sea surface temperature from archives located between 20 and 90° N.
steps.searchable_summary: select temperature reconstructions from specified archives

steps.description: Calculate anomalies between the period of interest and the reference period for each selected record.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate temperature anomalies for selected records

steps.description: Average reconstructions representing the same variable and period located in the same model grid.
steps.searchable_summary: average similar reconstructions in same grid

steps.description: Estimate uncertainty for each reconstruction for data assimilation.
